{{Modding tool
|type=Shared Mod Menu
|author=Conner
|status=Upcoming
|github=conner-olsen/eu5-community-mod-menu
}}

The Community Mod Menu (CMM) is a dependency mod that allows multiple mods to hook into a single shared setting menu through a simple registration API.

The guide below will be based on the CMM Example Mod, which can be found [TODO here on the workshop] or [https://github.com/conner-olsen/eu5-community-mod-menu/tree/main/submods/example-mod here on GitHub], where <code>example-mod</code> is the root mod folder.

== Basic Setup ==
First, create an effect file such as <code>example-mod/in_game/common/scripted_effects/cmm_example_menu_registration_effects.txt</code> Within it, use the <code>cmm_register_mod</code> effect to register your mod with a unique ID, such as:
<syntaxhighlight lang="text">
cmm_example_register_mod = { # The setup effect you create
	cmm_register_mod = {
		mod_id = cmm_example # Your unique setting ID
	}
}
</syntaxhighlight>
* <code>cmm_example_register_mod</code>, this is your registration effect and can be named whatever you want.
* <code>cmm_example</code> is the unique mod ID that you will choose.

Next, you will need to hook into the shared <code>on_action</code>. Create an <code>on_action</code> file in <code>example-mod/in_game/common/on_action/cmm_example_on_action.txt</code> and inside it, you will need to connect to the CMM on_action, such as:
<syntaxhighlight lang="text">
cmm_on_mod_registration = {
	on_actions = {
		cmm_example_on_register_mod # The on_action you create below.
	}
}

cmm_example_on_register_mod = {
	effect = {
		cmm_example_register_mod = yes # The registration effect you created previously.
	}
}
</syntaxhighlight>
* <code>cmm_on_mod_registration</code>: This is the <code>on_action</code> provided by CMM, and is how CMM will know to run your registration effect.
* <code>cmm_example_on_register_mod</code> is your on_action that will run your registration effect and can be named whatever you want.
* <code>cmm_example_register_mod</code> is the effect that we created above, and must match the name you chose before.

That is all that is required for your mod to show up in the menu. But now you will want to add localization so the mod's name is displayed instead of its ID.

Create a localization file such as <code>example-mod/main_menu/localization/english/cmm_example_mod_l_english.yml</code>, inside it you will want to create keys for your mod name and description, for the above example it would be:
<syntaxhighlight lang="yaml">
l_english:
 cmm_example_name: "CMM Example Mod"
 cmm_example_desc: "Reference integration mod for Community Mod Menu."
</syntaxhighlight>
* The key names are based on the unique mod ID you choose, with <code>[you-mod-id]_name</code> setting the name that appears in the searchbar and menu title, and <code>[your-mod-id]_desc</code> appearing as the tooltip when you hover over it.

Now, as long as CMM is enabled and loaded above your mod, you can access your mod menu through the shared interface.

== Creating a checkbox ==
You can create a checkbox using <code>cmm_register_bool_setting</code>, which you must place inside of the registration effect we created before such as:
<syntaxhighlight lang="text">
cmm_example_register_mod = {
	cmm_register_mod = {
		mod_id = cmm_example
	}

	cmm_register_bool_setting = {
		mod_id = cmm_example        # Matches your registered mod ID
		setting_id = example_toggle # Unique setting ID
		tab_id = general            # Target tab ID
		group_id = general_toggles  # Target group ID
		default_value = 0           # 0 = Unchecked, 1 = Checked
	}
}
</syntaxhighlight>
* <code>mod_id</code>: Must match the unique mod ID you registered with above.
* <code>setting_id</code>: Unique identifier for the this toggle, can be whatever you want.
* <code>tab_id</code>: Tab your setting will be placed under, can be whatever you want.
* <code>group_id</code>: Group your setting will be placed in, can be whatever you want.
* <code>default_value</code>: Default value of the checkbox, where 0 is unchecked and 1 is checked.

Then you must make a Scripted GUI file in order to assign a effect to the checkbox, such as <code>example-mod/in_game/common/scripted_guis/cmm_example_scripted_gui.txt</code>, and inside you will create the handler for the toggle like:
<syntaxhighlight lang="text">
cmm_example__example_toggle_on_changed = {
	scope = country

	effect = {
		cmm_toggle_bool_setting = {
			setting = cmm_example__example_toggle
		}
	}
}
</syntaxhighlight>
* <code>cmm_example__example_toggle_on_changed</code>: Will run whenever the checkbox is clicked, it must be named based your mod and setting ID you defined previously: <code>[Your_Mod_ID]__[Your_Setting_ID]_on_changed</code>
* <code>cmm_toggle_bool_setting</code>: A CMM provided helper that will handle the setting change.
* <code>setting</code>: You must provide the setting ID, which is always <code>[Your_Mod_ID]__[Your_Setting_ID]</code>, and thus for the example mod is <code>cmm_example__example_toggle</code>.

That is all that is needed in order to create your checkbox.

The value of the checkbox is synced with a country variable named <code>[Your_Mod_ID]__[Your_Setting_ID]</code>, with 1 for checked, and 0 for unchecked.
Elsewhere in your mod you can access the value of this variable through <code>var:[Your_Mod_ID]__[Your_Setting_ID]</code>.

For example if you wanted to determine whether a effect can be run for a nation:
<syntaxhighlight lang="text">
your_effect = {
	if = {
		limit = {
			var:cmm_example__example_toggle = 1
		}
		# Your effect logic here
	}
}
</syntaxhighlight>
Where the above would only run if the checkbox is checked.

{{Modding navbox
|category=Modding tools
}}
